NVIDIA (NVDA)

Source: Nvidia.xlsx · Analyzed: 2026-04-03 07:30 · Last actual: FY2026 (Jan 2026)

NVIDIA (NVDA)

Global leader in GPU-accelerated computing. Dominant AI infrastructure supplier.

Business Model

  • Data Center (~85%+ of revenue) — AI training and inference GPUs (H100, B100, Blackwell), networking (Mellanox/InfiniBand), and DGX systems. Sold to hyperscalers (Microsoft, Meta, Google, Amazon), sovereign AI, and enterprises.
  • Gaming (~10%) — GeForce desktop and notebook GPUs for consumers and PC gamers.
  • Automotive (~1-2%) — DRIVE platform for autonomous driving and infotainment.
  • Professional Visualization (~2-3%) — Quadro/RTX GPUs for design, engineering, and content creation.

Key Drivers

  • Data Center compute revenue — unit volumes and blended ASP of GPU accelerators (H100 → Blackwell transition)
  • Cloud CapEx cycle — hyperscaler capital spending directly drives DC GPU demand
  • Gross margin — product mix shift (DC GPUs carry higher margins), supply chain leverage
  • Networking revenue — Mellanox/InfiniBand growing with scale-out AI clusters
  • Gaming cycle — desktop vs notebook mix, ASP trends, crypto mining demand (CMP)

Model Flow

  • Worksheet — main operating model: consolidated P&L, segment revenue breakdown (Gaming, DC, Auto, ProViz), gross margin by segment, geographic splits, customer concentration
  • NVDA — detailed financial statements: P&L, balance sheet, cash flow, unit economics
  • Datacenter — DC compute revenue buildup: cloud capex tracking (AMZN, META, GOOGL, ORCL), GPU unit/ASP by architecture
  • Gaming — desktop/notebook GPU units, ASPs, crypto mining revenue
  • Automotive — autonomous driving + infotainment revenue buildup

Currency

USD (millions). Fiscal year ends January (e.g. FY2025 = Feb 2024 – Jan 2025).
